response.justification: Sarah Smith is a strong general-purpose software engineer with deep TypeScript and full-stack experience, but she does not meet the core requirements of this role. The job demands 8+ years of production-grade AI solution delivery; her AI exposure is limited to her current role starting April 2025, covering MCP tooling and agentic workflow infrastructure — valuable but nowhere near the required depth or tenure. Python is conspicuously absent from her skills despite being a primary required language. There is no mention of vector databases, RAG architectures, LLM evaluation pipelines, or customer-facing technical leadership in an AI consulting context. Her background is traditional software engineering, devops, and geospatial platforms rather than applied GenAI delivery to enterprise customers.
strengths: Strong TypeScript and Node.js depth with 10+ years of production use across multiple companies
Early-mover exposure to Model Context Protocol (MCP) infrastructure and agentic workflow frameworks at re:cinq
Staff-level track record and demonstrated ability to mentor, run workshops, and drive engineering practices
concerns: No Python listed anywhere in skills or employment history, despite it being a primary required language
AI experience is limited to ~1 month of current role; far short of the 8-year minimum for production-grade AI solutions
No evidence of vector databases, RAG architectures, LLM evaluation/observability, or enterprise customer-facing technical leadership
